mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and healthy economic expansion, which typically translates into strong corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals potential erosion of purchasing power and corporate profit margins, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative inflation signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer demand and low borrowing costs, creating an optimal environment for corporate growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect of RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS level does not show a statistically significant positive correlation with SPX 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic recession, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 6-month period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE shows a significant increase.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong investment in residential construction and consumer confidence, which typically precedes broader economic growth and positive equity market performance over short horizons.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 40% of periods where HOUST growth meets the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Steady expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling robust economic fundamentals that support corporate earnings and drive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO growth against SPX 20-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a monetary tightening cycle by the central bank, which raises the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, thereby depressing equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the FEDFUNDS increase.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment (UNRATE) and strong retail sales (RSAFS) signifies a robust consumer-driven economy, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of low UNRATE and strong RSAFS does not lead to significantly higher SPX 5-day returns compared to periods where only one condition is met (t-test p > 0.05).
